condition: status != 'entered-in-error' AND status != 'cancelled' AND status != 'declined' AND status != 'stopped'
narrative_md: Pharmacy dispensing events. One FHIR MedicationDispense produces one OMOP drug_exposure row with drug_type_concept_id = 38000175 (Prescription dispensed in pharmacy). Date is taken from whenHandedOver (fallback whenPrepared). End date is computed from start + daysSupply, or defaults to start date. No reviewed reference implementation covers this mapping; the spec is reconstructed from OMOP drug_exposure schema and FHIR R4 MedicationDispense definition.

fields.notes: Surrogate key, generated deterministically from MedicationDispense.id
Resolve Patient reference to person_id
RxNorm / ATC / NDC -> OMOP standard concept. Resolve medicationCodeableConcept or medicationReference
The date medication was supplied. Fallback to whenPrepared if whenHandedOver absent
Full ISO datetime
If daysSupply present: end = start + daysSupply. Otherwise fallback to start date
Null if no daysSupply
Leave null. MedicationDispense does not carry a verbatim end date
Prescription dispensed in pharmacy. Alternative: 32817 (EHR) used by FhirToCdm/ETL-German for other medication resources

sources.comment: This project and OMOP CDM guidance use 38000175 (Prescription dispensed in pharmacy) — the semantically precise type for a dispense event
FhirToCdm and ETL-German use 32817 (EHR) universally across all medication resource types; neither implements MedicationDispense but their pattern would apply

references.notes: Type concept 32817 used for all medication resources (line 338)

fields.notes: Leave null. MedicationDispense does not carry a discontinuation reason
Leave null. Refills are tracked on the originating MedicationRequest, not the dispense
Dispensed quantity (the total handed over)
Days of medication supplied. Direct FHIR field on MedicationDispense (unlike MedicationRequest where it must be computed)
Free-text dosage instructions. Fallback to patientInstruction
SNOMED route codes -> OMOP Route domain
Raw route text or coding[0].display
Not used for MedicationDispense
The dispensing pharmacist or actor
Encounter reference. Resolve to visit_occurrence_id
Leave null
Best code by vocabulary priority (RxNorm > ATC > NDC > SNOMED)
Source vocabulary concept. Placeholder: 0
Raw dose unit string from doseQuantity.unit or .code

entries.notes: Alternative type_concept_id used by some implementations universally

references.notes: Implements MedicationRequest + MedicationStatement only, NOT MedicationDispense
Implements MedicationRequest + Immunization only, NOT MedicationDispense
Implements MedicationStatement + MedicationAdministration only, NOT MedicationDispense
Implements MedicationRequest + MedicationAdministration only, NOT MedicationDispense

edge_cases.handling: Skip -- do not create drug_exposure row. Consensus across implementations for all medication resources.
Skip. No reference implementation precedent for MedicationDispense specifically. Recommended: skip non-completed dispenses.
Fallback to context (encounter) start date, or null. Consider dropping the record if no date can be resolved.
End date = start date. days_supply column left null.
Take first entry. Consistent with handling in other medication resources (MedicationRequest, MedicationStatement).

edge_cases.case: Cross-reference to originating MedicationRequest via authorizingPrescription
edge_cases.handling: Currently no implementation links these for OMOP. Could be used to inherit dosage/refill metadata in future.

edge_cases.case: Medication resolution: medicationReference to contained or external Medication
edge_cases.handling: Resolve contained Medication inline. For external references, look up by reference. Most implementations handle inline medicationCodeableConcept only.
Pick best by vocabulary priority: RxNorm > ATC > NDC > SNOMED.
OMOP varchar(50) limit. Truncate to 50 characters.
